Hi Carlos. Can you hear me clearly?
Yes, I can hear you. Can I share my screen now?
Sure. Could you present the audit criteria?
Of course. I can present them and explain the main points.
Sorry, could you repeat the last point, please?
Yes, I can repeat it. There are eleven critical criteria.
Great. Can you send me the checklist after the meeting?
Yes, I can send it today. I can also schedule the next call.
Can you hear me clearly?
Can I share my screen?
I can explain the main criteria.

Let me tell you what I can do in an online meeting. I can open the call and check the sound. I can share my screen and I can present the audit criteria. I can explain the main points in simple English. When the connection is bad, I can ask my colleague to repeat. I can also send documents and schedule the next call. There is one thing I can't do well yet: I can't translate every technical term alone. But I can ask for help, and that is enough. That is my job in the meeting.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma with a quick note before our call. Could you share your screen at the start? I want to see the audit criteria. Could you also explain the two critical points? If I speak too fast, please ask me to repeat. Can you send me the updated checklist after the meeting? And could you schedule the next call for Friday morning? Thank you, Carlos. You can do this. See you soon.

First, Carlos opens the online meeting. He asks: Can you hear me clearly? Then he shares his screen. He can present the audit criteria. He asks his colleague: Could you send me the checklist? At the end, they schedule the next call.
